title: Literals should not be thrown
description: Literals should not be thrownAnalyze your codeconsistency-conventionalmaintainabilityCode SmellQuick FixIDE quick fixes available with SonarQube for IDEerror-handlingapi-designtype-dependentWhy is this an issue?More InfoIn JavaScript, throwing literals (primitive values like strings, numbers, booleans, etc.) as exceptions is generally discouraged. While it is syntactically valid to throw literals, it is considered a best practice to throw instances of theErrorclass or its subclasses instead.Throwing an instance of theErrorclass allows you to provide more meaningful information about the error.TheErrorclass and its subclasses provide properties likemessageandstackthat can be used to convey useful details about the error, such as a description of the problem, the context in which it occurred
recommendation: Replace with the recommended alternative approach.

checks.message: Literals should not be thrown - use Error objects instead
